blockedAssertions.blockedBy: A compose instrumentation test verifies the cancel path against SnippetFakeSessionController, but no runnable real-SSH fixture test was found that proves an unchanged remote transcript and absence of a success record on the real 10.0.2.2:3122 target.

Current snippet instrumentation previews saved-host targeting and latest content, but no runnable fixture-backed flow was found that dispatches a multiline snippet to a selected saved host and proves ordered remote output on the real SSH fixture.

The available active-session execution proof uses SnippetFakeSessionController rather than the real SSH fixture, so it does not satisfy the contract requirement to prove reuse of the actual live session transcript.
Source inspection found no runnable snippet instrumentation that changes or removes the live session and then re-validates the snippet target surface against the real fixture.
A fake-controller test disables the confirm button after the first tap, but no runnable real-SSH test was found that proves only one remote execution reaches the fixture transcript.

Failure and unproven-payload handling are covered only with SnippetFakeSessionController; no runnable fixture-backed comparison case was found to prove the real SSH transcript gates recent-history success entries.
Recent-history ordering and target labels are covered in compose instrumentation, but the available proof does not use the real fixture target required for snippet-history validation.

History persistence, latest-content preview, and deleted-entry stability are covered with seeded history state, but no runnable real-SSH execution flow was found that proves those behaviors from an actual successful fixture-backed run.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that VAL-SNIPPET-008 through VAL-SNIPPET-015 currently lack runnable real-SSH fixture instrumentation and remain covered only by fake-controller or seeded-history tests.
